div.head:
No. 15.
Earl Granville to Mr. West.
div.p: Sir: With reference to my dispatch to Mr. Drummond of the 9th July last, I transmit herewith for your information, copies of a further correspondence I have had with the legation of the United States, in this country, relative to the arrest of Mr. Joseph B. Walsh, who has been imprisoned at Kilmainham under the protection of person and property act in Ireland, 1881, and has now been released by direction of the lord-lieutenant of Ireland, on the ground of the ill-health of the prisoner.Nos. 12 and 14.
